sections.paragraph:
προσομιλέω 1 aor. 3 sg. προσωμίλησε(ν) (Just.); inf. προσομιλῆσαι (Eur., Thu.+; Just.; Tat. 5, 2; in a difft. sense Ath., R. 5 p. 53, 10) to communicate conversationally, speak to, converse with τινί someone (Theognis 1, 31 κακοῖσι μὴ προσομίλει ἀνδράσιν; Pla., Gorg. 502e; Vett. Val. 353, 1; Philo, Agr. 60; Just., D. 1, 2 al.) of communication by letter IEph 9:2.—DELG s.v. ὅμιλος.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

inf. inf. = infinitive

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

difft. difft. = different(ly)

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Theognis Theognis , VI b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Agr Agr = Agraphon (non-canonical sayings of Jesus)—List 1

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 883
